parties.name: LOPES DEMASI, JULIANA LOURENCO 45 LANSING STREET SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94109

calendar.matters: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Motion To Compel Investigation Regarding Patent Rights And Collusion
Off Calendar Jun-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Juliana Lourenco Lopes Demasi's Motion To Compel Investigation Regarding Patent Rights And Collusion is DENIED.

Plaintiff is admonished to review and comply with the Rules of Court regarding document formatting, content and length. Plaintiff's papers include numerous violations of the relevant rules. Non-compliant filings may be stricken.

Plaintiff fails to identify any legal authority to support her request or the orders she requests. Plaintiff may conduct and respond to discovery consistent with the Civil Discovery Act. Plaintiff must address any issues or concerns regarding Orders to Show Cause to the department or bench officers that issued the orders. This department and bench officer has not issued any orders to show cause to Plaintiff in this action.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Juliana Lourenco Lopes Demasi's unopposed Motion To Compel Discovery is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiff failed timely to file proper proof of service of her moving papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) The court will not consider the merits of an unopposed motion absent timely submission of proof that the moving papers were properly served. Additionally, Moving Party failed to identify in her motion a legal basis for the motion. Proceedings in this court in this action are not governed by the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.
